one_line_takeaway: By optimizing orthogonal weights directly on the manifold (with a cheap Taylor-approximated exponential) and replacing convolutions with parameter-free norm-preserving spatial shifts, LipNeXt pushes deterministic Lipschitz-based certified robustness to billion-parameter models with non-saturating, SOTA certified accuracy.
key_finding: LipNeXt sets new state-of-the-art clean and certified robust accuracy across CIFAR-10/100 and Tiny-ImageNet, and -- crucially -- scales Lipschitz certification to 1-2B parameters on ImageNet with NON-saturating gains, improving CRA over prior Lipschitz models by up to +8% at epsilon=1 and +3% at epsilon=36/255, while training stably in bfloat16 with throughput on par with much smaller prior models.
core_contribution: LipNeXt is a 1-Lipschitz neural architecture for DETERMINISTIC certified adversarial robustness (l2-norm) that is both 'constraint-free' and 'convolution-free', enabling Lipschitz-based certification to scale to billion-parameter models for the first time. Its two own innovations are: (1) a constraint-free manifold-optimization scheme (Stabilized Manifold Adam with a norm-adaptive Taylor 'FastExp' approximation of the matrix exponential, plus per-epoch SVD polar retraction and a tangent-space Lookahead) that learns orthogonal weights directly on the orthogonal/Stiefel manifold instead of re-parameterizing them (as SOC, Cayley, LOT, Cholesky do) or approximating orthogonality (AOL/CPL/SLL); and (2) a parameter-free Spatial Shift Module that replaces depthwise convolutions for spatial mixing, justified by their Theorem 1 (a circular-padding depthwise conv is exactly norm-preserving iff its kernel has a single +-1 entry, i.e. it reduces to a spatial shift). These are assembled into a LiResNet-style macro-architecture using orthogonal channel projections R/R^T, the Spatial Shift, a tunable 1-Lipschitz beta-Abs activation (which subsumes MinMax at beta=0.5), and an L2 Spatial Pool.
threat_model: Worst-case l2-bounded adversarial perturbations: an adversary may perturb input x by any x' with ||x - x'||_2 <= epsilon. The defense goal is CERTIFIED (deterministic, provable) robustness -- a mathematical guarantee that the classifier's prediction is unchanged for the entire epsilon-ball, giving a per-input certified radius |f(x)|/K where K upper-bounds the network's Lipschitz constant. The paper explicitly contrasts this deterministic guarantee with randomized smoothing (RS), arguing RS's probabilistic certificates admit false positives (adversarial examples wrongly certified robust, typically <0.1% but nonzero) and cost thousands of forward passes per image; LipNeXt's certificates have no such false-positive risk. Motivating safety-critical settings cited: autonomous driving, medical imaging, malware classification.
limitations: Certified radii remain modest in absolute terms (e.g. CIFAR-10 CRA drops to ~43-46% at eps=108/255; ImageNet CRA at eps=1 is ~22%). At a single point (CIFAR-10 CRA at eps=108/255) AOL still beats LipNeXt, though at a large clean-accuracy cost. Scaling up model size without extra data triggers robust overfitting (clean accuracy rises but CRA falls), requiring diffusion-generated synthetic data to fix. The scaling-law study (Table 4) uses only a 400-class ImageNet subset due to compute limits. FastExp thresholds (0.05/0.25/1) and shift/beta hyperparameters are set empirically. Only l2-norm robustness is addressed (no l-infinity). Circular padding is required for the norm-preservation theorem; the authors had to add positional encoding to recover the accuracy benefit that zero-padding otherwise gives.

datasets_used: Diffusion-generated (EDM) synthetic data as extra training data
quantitative_results: CIFAR-10, LipNeXt L32W2048 (256M params), no extra data: Clean 85.0%, CRA 73.2% @eps=36/255, 58.8% @72/255, 43.3% @108/255
CIFAR-10, LipNeXt L32W1024 (64M): Clean 81.5%, CRA 71.2/59.2/45.9% at 36/72/108 (per 255); best prior BRONet (68M): Clean 81.6%, CRA 70.6/57.2/42.5%
Tiny-ImageNet, LipNeXt L32W2048 (256M): Clean 45.5%, CRA 35.0/25.9/18.0%; BRONet (75M): Clean 41.2%, CRA 29.0/19.0/12.1%
With extra diffusion data, CIFAR-10, LipNeXt L32W2896 (512M): Clean 92.7%, CRA 81.7/68.6/55.8%; best prior BRO (68M): Clean 87.2%, CRA 78.3/67.4/54.5%
ImageNet, LipNeXt L32W5792 (2B): eps=1 Clean 41.0%/CRA 22.4%; eps=36/255 Clean 57.0%/CRA 41.2%
ImageNet, LipNeXt L32W4096 (1B): eps=1 Clean 40.2%/CRA 21.1%; eps=36/255 Clean 55.9%/CRA 40.3%; vs LiResNet (51M) eps=1 CRA 14.2% -> roughly +8% at eps=1
Training speed (min/epoch, normalized to single 8xH100 node, bfloat16): LipNeXt 1B = 8.9, 2B = 17.8; LiResNet (51M) = 5.3, BRONet (86M) = 10.5
Scaling on ImageNet-400 at eps=1 (fixed depth 32): width 1024->4096 raises Clean 40.5->51.7% and CRA 22.9->30.0%; under fixed 1B params, depth=32 is optimal (Clean 51.7/CRA 30.0)
